mock-123.title: Q3 Investor Pitch
live_notes: Opening sentence lands clearly, but pace is above target in first 15 seconds.
Two filler clusters detected near the product framing transition.
Eye contact remains strong on key claims, then drifts when recalling figures.
Shoulders tighten slightly while introducing pricing assumptions.
Voice projection is steady, but sentence endings drop in volume.
Clear emphasis on customer pain points; this section sounds confident.
Hand gestures pause for ~20 seconds during the TAM explanation.
Breathing cadence normalizes after the first minute and pacing improves.
Posture remains upright with brief forward lean for emphasis.
Great pause before the roadmap section, increases clarity.
Eye-line returns to camera quickly after glance-away moments.

notes.title: Pace settled after opening
notes.body: You started fast in the first 30 seconds, then stabilized into a conversational rhythm.

notes.title: Working draft insight
notes.body: Coach is still synthesizing a complete clarity assessment.

notes.title: Strong close
notes.body: Your final minute was clear, concise, and confident.

notes.title: Revenue model explanation improved
notes.body: The revised phrasing around pricing tiers reduced ambiguity.

notes.title: Consistent eye contact
notes.body: Camera-facing posture and eye line were sustained for most of the talk.

sess_001.content: What should I improve first for my next rehearsal?
Prioritize pacing in your opening minute, then tighten transitions between financial points.
